summary:
During β-oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids, cells generate both acetyl CoA and specifically propionyl CoA; these findings are supported by multiple studies emphasizing distinct metabolism processes involved only when dealing with non-standard carbon chain lengths. To summarize concisely while focusing clinically significant aspects related directly back towards our original query about end products during oxidation cycles pertinent here is crucial information conveyed effectively within limited space provided initially set forth goals earlier discussed thoroughly above. Key takeaways include understanding how different types of fats undergo specialized breakdown mechanisms producing identifiable intermediaries which can then be utilized further downstream depending
